text:
Special $2 coins have been made available to celebrate the Australian athletes competing in the Paris Olympic and Paralympic Games.

Australians can now get their hands on new $2 coins which have been released to celebrate the nation's Olympic and Paralympic athletes competing in Paris.

The Royal Australian Mint have created four coloured uncirculated coins which are individually being made available at participating Woolworths supermarkets.

On Wednesday, the first of the coins were issued through the tills. It is dedicated to the Olympic Team and features the word 'community'.

Another Olympic Team coin featuring 'Allez Aus', the supporter chant for athletes in the Paris games, will be made available on July 31.

A third Olympic Team coin with the word 'Olympism' will be issued on August 14, while the Paralympic Team coin will be available on August 28.

All four coins have an individual design feature, alongside the Olympic-themed wording.

The Royal Australian Mint is also offering all Olympic Team coins available for purchase as a three-coin collection set.

The Paralympic Team coin can be purchased as a one-coin collection piece.

The Olympic Games will be held from July 26 to August 11, while the Paralympic Games will take place from August 28 to September 8.

Meanwhile, Australia Post confirmed on Wednesday it will release stamps commemorating Australian Olympic gold medal winners.

Australians can obtain the stamps online within 48 hours of the medal ceremony, and in post office outlets within one week.

"Recognising athletes on a stamp that is accessible to their families and all Australians is a special tribute," Australia Post Executive General Manager Retail, Josh Bannister, said.

"Whether it be in the pool, track, courtside, on the pitch or in the ring, Aussies can throw their support behind their favourite gold medallists and take home a piece of history with a stamp or coin, sharing the love and excitement for the Paris Olympics."

title: New $2 coins celebrating Australia's Olympic and Paralympic athletes released by Royal Australian Mint to mark Paris 2024 | Sky News Australia
